page.title: App-side openModal test page
page.description: This page keeps two integration paths for `DemoActionModal`: one through `openModal(ModalEnum.DemoActionModal)` and one through direct component mounting. It also keeps 2 app-side local modal components using `openModal`, so command-style and component-style usage can be verified side by side.
page.caseDescription: This area keeps both command-style and component-style tests for package `DemoActionModal`, alongside app-side component cases.

logs.initial: After opening any case, Promise resolve / reject results will be recorded here.
logs.demoAction: {action}, kind=demo-action, ticketId={ticketId}, status={status}, operator={operatorName}, remark={remark}, processedAt={processedAt}

mountedPackage.label: Package component mount
mountedPackage.summary: This case mounts the `DemoActionModal` component exported from `packages/components` directly on the page, verifying package modal usage as a component instead of opening it through `openModal`.

modal.title: Package built-in modal
modal.description: This case mounts the DemoActionModal component directly on the page to verify component-style integration instead of command-style opening through openModal.

packageImperative.label: Package command modal
packageImperative.summary: This keeps the command-style `openModal(ModalEnum.DemoActionModal)` case for comparison with the component mount below.

modal.title: Package command modal
modal.description: This case hits the package internal mapping through ModalEnum.DemoActionModal and verifies DemoActionModal with command-style usage.

metrics.label: Metrics snapshot modal
metrics.summary: Use a metric-card component to verify different layouts, `size/width/centered` passthrough, and card selection results.

modal.title: Size and layout passthrough
modal.heading: This opens a wider modal and disables vertical centering, making it easier to inspect how the app-side metric-card component lays out after integrating BaseModal.

checklist.label: Checklist confirm modal
checklist.summary: Use a checklist component to verify button prop passthrough and confirm that the page is using another distinct local modal implementation.

modal.title: Form validation state
modal.summary: This case focuses on the disabled-looking confirm button and danger button props, while also verifying the result returned by a checklist modal.

tips.order: 1. The queue button opens command-style `openModal` cases in order and switches between different app-side components.
tips.props: 2. The 3rd queued case uses a wider modal, making it easier to verify that props do not leak between components.
tips.logs: 3. The log area prints result summaries by `kind`, and the component mount case is recorded here as well.

metricsFirst.label: Queued metrics modal #1
metricsFirst.title: Queued metrics modal #1
metricsFirst.heading: The first queued modal uses the metric-card component to confirm that the first item in the openModal queue opens normally.

checklistSecond.label: Queued checklist modal #2
checklistSecond.title: Queued checklist modal #2
checklistSecond.summary: The second modal switches to a checklist component to confirm that switching component types inside the queue does not contaminate state.

metricsThird.label: Queued metrics modal #3
metricsThird.title: Queued metrics modal #3
metricsThird.heading: The third modal switches back to the metrics component and widens the modal to verify that props do not leak.
